frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: I get women being careful, but sometimes it feels like guys like us get judged for no reason yaar. Not every man is some threat, and if someone acts suspicious around every normal guy also then what are we supposed to do? Men also deal with plenty of random accusations and safety issues, but nobody talks about that.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear by centering male hurt and insisting that women are unfairly generalizing men. It encodes the 'not all men' response, demands trust from women, and deflects with 'men also suffer' rather than engaging the structural issue.
original_frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 27. Single. A man in Bengaluru. You are on a third date with a woman named Kavya who mentioned casually that most of her close friends are male Ñ she finds it easier, less drama, she has always been like this. You are back home texting your best friend about the date. You are telling him what Kavya said about her friend group and what you think it means and whether you see this as a red flag and what kind of woman has mostly male friends and whether you would pursue something serious with her.
